seoTitle: McKees Hill vs McKellars Park — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between McKees Hill and McKellars Park. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: McKees Hill vs McKellars Park: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in McKees Hill and McKellars Park.
heading: McKees Hill vs McKellars Park — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between McKees Hill, New South Wales and McKellars Park, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: McKees Hill has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to McKellars Park (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is McKees Hill or McKellars Park safer from flooding?
faq.answer: McKees Hill has a flood risk score of 33/100, while McKellars Park scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: McKees Hill scores 21/100 for bushfire risk. McKellars Park scores 33/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in McKees Hill or McKellars Park?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. McKees Hill has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.53% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
